methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Frankenburg Technologies is a Series A defense and security company founded by Taavi Madiberk and Marko Virkebau, having raised €40M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private companies in the Defense & Security sector at Series A/B stages, supplemented by 2 public comps (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS). Top private peers include Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

The peer-median method applies directly to this stage and sector. The analysis examined 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers with valuations ranging from $125M to $3.0B. The cohort median stands at $1.0B, reflected across companies including Castelion and Defense Unicorns at $1.0B, Code Metal at $1.2B, Harmattan AI at $1.4B, and Dream Security at $3.0B. This peer-median approach yields an implied valuation of $1.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $850M to $1.1B, with a median point of $1.0B. The analysis is constrained by reliance on a single applicable method; public comparables in this nascent frontier-tech segment show limited relevance due to different maturity profiles. Key uncertainties center on revenue traction and customer concentration relative to the peer set, neither of which is provided in available data. The $1.0B midpoint reflects peer-set positioning at Series A, though stage-specific discounts relative to Series B peers warrant consideration.
